messages.content: Vandry Surgical, Supplier Quality Engineer II. Fourteen days in conversation, A- fit, 85% match, ghost risk low, docs ready. The recruiter said this morning that the posting is live and I should submit through it.

Recruiter told you to submit, so conversation → applied. Fourteen days of back-and-forth with docs already ready is long enough. Apply through the live posting today and email the recruiter the confirmation — an A- at 85% should not get lost in an applicant queue.

All C fits. I am a clinical lab scientist in Madison moving into healthcare data analysis. Titles 'data analyst'. Keywords 'SQL, Power BI, Python'. Locations 'Madison'. I get generic analyst roles at insurance and marketing firms that want three years as an analyst.

Generic analyst titles hide the one thing that makes you competitive, which is that you already work inside a hospital. Change titles to 'healthcare data analyst, clinical informatics analyst, clinical data analyst' and add 'laboratory, Epic, HL7, turnaround time, HIPAA' to keywords. That flips your lab background from a gap into the qualification. Add 'Remote, Milwaukee' to locations. Three runs a day on that set. Health systems and specialty clinic networks run their own hiring pages and list there first, so check every system within an hour of Madison directly and use Add role for anything the scan missed.

Write the opening 1-2 sentences of a cover letter for the WMS Solutions Analyst role at Kettlebridge Depot. The posting describes their Sparks campus as three buildings running on one warehouse management instance, where cold-chain rules make every configuration change riskier than in dry goods. Hook on that specific detail.

Running three buildings on one WMS instance is hard enough before cold-chain rules make every configuration change riskier. That's the kind of environment where I'd want to work at Kettlebridge Depot.

Write a general resume summary for Anneke Ruzicka, Production Manager in Cedar Rapids, IA, leading with achievements. Use these facts: she raised line throughput 21% by rebalancing the changeover sequence and standardizing two shift-handoff routines, cut changeover time from 78 minutes to 34 using SMED across 7 format changes, and improved schedule attainment from 82% to 96% over three quarters.

Raised line throughput 21% by rebalancing the changeover sequence and standardizing two shift-handoff routines. Cut changeover time from 78 minutes to 34 using SMED across 7 format changes, and improved schedule attainment from 82% to 96% over three quarters. Consistent record of moving floor metrics through operator-level process changes.

I'm tailoring my resume for the VP of Operations opening at Ironbark Fulfillment. The posting wants experience with a peak season that more than doubles volume. Here's my bullet: "Kept every DC operating through a 6-week port disruption by pre-positioning inventory on a 90-day forward view." Sharpen it for this JD.

Kept every distribution center operating through a 6-week port disruption by pre-positioning inventory on a 90-day forward view — the kind of resilience this role's more-than-double peak season also demands.
Loftwren | Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics | applied | 27 days | B+ fit | 76% match | ghost risk high | docs tailored. The posting is off their careers page and the recruiter's mailbox bounces.

applied → closed. Nobody rejected you — the req is off the page and the recruiter's mailbox bounces, so there is no one left to make a decision. You end it. Twenty-seven days at high ghost risk had already told you this. The tailored pricing docs still travel.

Just three and I'm stuck on all of them.
Fenwick Housing Institute · Senior Research Associate, Housing Policy · interview · B+ · 80% · 5 days · low · yes
Foothill Community Health Alliance · Grants Manager · applied · A- · 85% · 24 days · high · yes
Ninebark Regional Water Authority · Capital Programs Director · researched · C · 63% · 11 days · medium · no

First, Fenwick Housing Institute — five days into an interview, B+/80%, docs already tailored. Prepare for it; nothing else here has a date attached.
Second, Foothill Community Health Alliance. A-/85% is the better fit, and it still ranks below an interview: 24 days applied at high ghost risk means the file is cold. Send one follow-up and treat silence as an answer.
Third, Ninebark Regional Water Authority. C/63%, eleven days researched, no docs — a capital programs directorship is a different career, not a next step.
Drop Ninebark. Then source two more housing-policy roles; a three-role board can't absorb a single no.

For my Rowanmere Health Technologies Director of Client Experience cover letter, write one proof-point paragraph connecting this fact — I cut time-to-value for new enterprise accounts from 74 days to 38 through a redesigned onboarding program — to the posting's requirement for designing an onboarding program that shortens time-to-value in a measurable way.

Rowanmere wants time-to-value shortened in a measurable way, and that's precisely what a redesigned onboarding program did on my team: 74 days down to 38 for new enterprise accounts. That's the kind of measurable result I'd bring to Rowanmere's implementation handoff.
Quillbase, Director of Product, Automation — interview stage, A fit, 90% match, product-sense panel 11 days ago, ghost risk medium, tailored docs yes.

Eleven days after a product-sense panel with nothing back means the loop stalled somewhere internal, not on you. Write the recruiter Monday and ask a question they can actually answer: whether the role is still moving and what the next step would be. Skip any restatement of your enthusiasm — at an A and 90 percent, that's established. One message, then two weeks of quiet before you consider another.

Grade this candidate against this role. Return ONLY JSON, no other text: {"score":int,"summary":"...","strengths":[...],"gaps":[...]}

ROLE: Tessellate Software - VP, Customer Success (VP; construction project accounting for general contractors; $90M ARR)
NEEDS: led a CS organisation of 25+; ownership of a gross retention number; moved retention where churn had structural causes; CSM segmentation and capacity models; Gainsight as system of record; carried or designed an expansion quota; vertical software preferred; hire two directors; present retention to the board quarterly
CANDIDATE: Simone Aguilar - Head of Customer Success.
- Leads a 31-person customer success organisation covering $74M ARR
- Gross revenue retention 87% to 94% across a 900-account book
- Rebuilt onboarding, time-to-value 74 days to 38
- Health-scoring model predicts churn 90 days out at 71%
- Segment pods lifted CSM capacity 22 to 34 accounts
- Expansion revenue $4.1M to $12.6M after a CSM quota; no board exposure
SKILLS: Gainsight, segmentation strategy

{"score":83,"summary":"She has run this job at slightly smaller scale and moved the number that matters. Board exposure and the vertical are the gaps.","strengths":["A 31-person customer success organisation clears the stated 25+ threshold","Gross retention ownership is explicit: 87% to 94% across a 900-account book","Churn had structural causes; onboarding rebuilt, time-to-value 74 days to 38","Segmentation and capacity modelling proven: segment pods lifted CSM capacity 22 to 34","Gainsight is core, health scoring predicts churn 90 days out at 71%, and expansion grew $4.1M to $12.6M"],"gaps":["No board presentation experience; this role reports retention to the board quarterly","Construction project accounting and general contractors are outside her record; vertical software preferred","Her book is $74M against a $90M business, and hiring two directors is untested"]}

Write a resume summary for Yolanda Prieto, Grants and Contracts Administrator, in Albuquerque, NM. Use only these facts: she monitors 23 subrecipients including annual risk assessments and 9 on-site reviews a year, and she trained 40 program staff on Uniform Guidance allowability rules. Make it leadership-led, 2-4 sentences.

Grants and Contracts Administrator who extends compliance oversight beyond her own desk. Monitors 23 subrecipients, including annual risk assessments and 9 on-site reviews a year. Trained 40 program staff on Uniform Guidance allowability rules, building compliance capacity across the department.

Parse the posting below into JSON — company, title, seniority, location, requirements. Just the JSON object, no explanation.

Ready to apply? Click below.

Sunderly is hiring: Principal Product Manager, Platform APIs
Location: Remote (US)
Level: Senior individual contributor

What you'll need:
- Written communication that stands on its own, because half this company is asynchronous
- SQL and analytics fluency; we expect PMs to pull their own adoption numbers
- Prior work with a developer relations or solutions engineering function is a plus

More jobs at this company →

Grade this candidate against this role. Return ONLY JSON, no other text: {"score":int,"summary":"...","strengths":[...],"gaps":[...]}

ROLE: Petrichor Beverage Company - Logistics Manager (beverage plants shipping to distributors and club retail)
NEEDS: four to eight years across inbound and outbound freight; rate negotiation with truckload carriers and freight brokers; temperature-sensitive or beverage freight incl. pallet economics; freight audit and payment, catching the invoices that are wrong; Excel modelling; retail vendor compliance and chargeback avoidance, ideally a club or mass customer; trailer utilisation on the mixed-SKU distributor lanes
CANDIDATE: Idris Bahri - Transportation Manager.
- Outbound truckload and LTL for 2 distribution centres
- Rebid of 38 lanes negotiated carrier-direct, no brokers; trailer utilisation 74% to 88%
- Cut empty miles 18% by pairing inbound backhauls with outbound lanes; no beverage freight
SKILLS: rate negotiation, LTL and truckload, freight audit and payment, Excel modelling

{"score":66,"summary":"Solid outbound freight credentials with two real holes: this is beverage in temperature-sensitive lanes, and it carries retail vendor compliance he has never handled.","strengths":["Four to eight years across freight, outbound truckload and LTL under him today","Rate negotiation with truckload carriers is proven through the 38-lane rebid","Freight audit and payment is listed, matching the requirement to catch wrong invoices","Excel modelling is listed, and he cut empty miles 18% pairing inbound backhauls with outbound lanes","Trailer utilisation transfers to the mixed-SKU distributor lanes named here"],"gaps":["No temperature-sensitive or beverage freight; pallet economics are called out","Retail vendor compliance and chargeback avoidance with a club or mass customer are absent","Broker relationships are unevidenced; his procurement is carrier-direct"]}

Quick fit check. My role: Warehouse Systems Analyst, 780,000 square-foot fulfillment center, 52,000 units a day. I led the labor management system configuration giving the site engineered standards on 14 activity types, reduced cycle-count variance from 1.9% to 0.4% by moving to daily ABC counting, and ran the wave-planning redesign that cut late-cutoff order miss rate from 3.1% to 0.6%. I documented 22 SOPs that became the template for two sister sites. The job: WMS Solutions Analyst at Kettlebridge Depot, Sparks, NV - configuring WMS changes in staging before they hit the floor, taking over reporting, and being first escalation for scanning and conveyor issues.

Direct match: your wave-planning redesign and cycle-count accuracy work map exactly to their task-interleaving and inventory accuracy requirements, and writing 22 SOPs that became a template for two sister sites shows the documentation habit this role needs for training supervisors. Your labor management system configuration also covers ground they value. Gap: their environment is cold-chain and lot-tracking specific, which your background doesn't show experience with, though the JD notes they'll train for it. Confirm availability for occasional weekend cutovers and second-shift reachability, both explicitly required.
Northfell Capital Partners, VP Risk Analytics. Interview stage, 16 days, A- fit, 87% match. Third round finished Tuesday and HR emailed asking for comp expectations and two references.

References plus a comp question after round three is the offer motion. interview → offer once the number arrives - send both references today so nothing waits on you. At A-/87% after sixteen days, answer with a range, not a single figure.

Board:
Northmarch Consumer Brands — Category Manager, Marketplace — interview — A- — 85% — 6 days — low — yes
Halvard & Pike — Senior Manager, Digital Merchandising — conversation — A — 89% — 4 days — low — no
Wickham Row — Director of E-commerce Operations — applied — B+ — 78% — 21 days — medium — yes
Bramble and Rye Hospitality Group — Operations Analytics Analyst — sourced — C — 51% — 28 days — high — no
Bellrose Outfitters — Retail Operations Analyst — sourced — C — 47% — 35 days — high — no

Which three today?

Halvard & Pike, first thing. It's your best fit at A/89%, only four days into the conversation, and there are no tailored docs — a merchandising lead will ask for something in writing and you have nothing.
Northmarch next: six days after the interview, A-/85%. A specific follow-up, referencing what they pushed on.
Wickham Row third — 21 days applied, medium ghost risk, docs already sent. One direct note, then leave it.
The two C-grade analyst roles have gone a month untouched at high risk. They're not coming back.

What's actually worth my Saturday? Kids are out of the house until six.
Osterby Clinical Communications | Associate Medical Writer | conversation | B+ | 80% | 2 days | low | docs no
Emberline Health | Clinical Content Lead | applied | A- | 85% | 13 days | medium | docs yes
Larkfield Health System | Healthcare Data Analyst | rejected | A | 90% | 6 days | high | docs yes
Wexley Care Partners | Operations Manager, Home Infusion | interview | B | 72% | 8 days | low | docs yes

Give Saturday to Osterby Clinical Communications. Two days into a conversation, B+/80%, and no tailored docs — that's the one place six free hours changes the outcome.
Second, Wexley Care Partners. It's an interview and it deserves real prep, but it ranks below Osterby: a B/72% operations job you'd take out of habit shouldn't get your best hours.
Third, Emberline Health — A-/85%, 13 days applied, medium risk. Ten minutes for a follow-up, no more.
Fourth, Larkfield Health System. Rejected at A/90%, which stings; the docs are already built, so reuse them for Osterby.
Nothing to drop. Four roles is already lean.

Pasting a JD below. Respond with just the JSON — keys should be company, title, seniority, location, requirements. No markdown fences, no commentary.

Job code: 79290-AUTOREQ

Halewood Bancorp is hiring: Director, Regulatory Affairs
Location: Denver, CO — Hybrid, 3 days on-site
Career level: Director

What we're looking for:
- Roughly eight years across bank or nonbank regulatory compliance, with meaningful time at director or deputy level
- Deep familiarity with the consumer regulatory perimeter: fair lending, UDAAP, complaint management, marketing review
- Ability to write for a board audience without losing the legal substance

« Return to search results

{"company": "Halewood Bancorp", "title": "Director, Regulatory Affairs", "seniority": "Director", "location": "Denver, CO — Hybrid, 3 days on-site", "requirements": ["Roughly eight years across bank or nonbank regulatory compliance, with meaningful time at director or deputy level", "Deep familiarity with the consumer regulatory perimeter: fair lending, UDAAP, complaint management, marketing review", "Ability to write for a board audience without losing the legal substance"]}

Trying to punch up this bullet for a Vice President, Risk Analytics application at Northfell Capital Partners: "Manages 12 analysts and data scientists across strategy, modelling and portfolio monitoring." They're consolidating three fractured analytics groups into one function. Give me a stronger version.
Manages 12 analysts and data scientists across strategy, modelling and portfolio monitoring — team-building at the scale this VP role needs to merge three fractured analytics groups into one.

Rank these. I teach until 3:40 so evenings are all I have.
- Prairiestone Credit Group · Data Analyst I, Lending · applied · A · 88% · 5 days · low · docs yes
- Cordwain Financial · Analyst, Portfolio Reporting · researched · A- · 84% · 3 days · low · docs no
- Ardent Ledger Group · Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance · sourced · C · 55% · 17 days · high · docs no
- Ashgrove Mutual Insurance · Associate Pricing Analyst · conversation · B+ · 79% · 8 days · medium · docs no
- Coppervane Financial · Compliance Manager, Installment Lending · sourced · C · 61% · 12 days · high · docs no

Prairiestone Credit Group holds first: A/88%, applied five days ago, docs already tailored. It's the entry-level lending analyst seat that makes the whole switch legible.
Ashgrove Mutual Insurance second — a live conversation at eight days, B+/79%, but no docs. That's tonight's work.
Cordwain Financial third: A-/84%, three days researched, still needs tailoring.
Fourth Coppervane Financial, fifth Ardent Ledger Group. Tiebreak: both are C-grade and stale, so the higher match ranks first.
Drop both. A senior manager and a compliance manager title with no analyst record behind you is a 55% and a 61% for a reason. Evenings are too scarce to spend reaching.

I want a straight read on this one. My background: E-commerce Merchandising Manager, $118M digital sales, 24,000 SKUs. I lifted conversion from 2.4% to 3.3%, ran 46 A/B tests with 17 shipped (including one adding $2.1M in revenue), and manage 4 merchandisers. Skills: category management, CRO, A/B testing, PIM, GA4, Adobe Commerce, cross-sell strategy. The job is Director of E-commerce Operations at Wickham Row - a 140-store specialty apparel retailer, needing 8+ years across retail or e-commerce operations with direct omnichannel fulfillment experience, building one shared scorecard for digital and field leadership.

Partial fit at best: your A/B testing rigor and the assortment scorecard you built for buy reviews show you can build the kind of shared scorecard this role wants across digital and field leadership. But the JD's core ask is direct omnichannel fulfillment experience - ship-from-store, BOPIS, curbside - which isn't part of your merchandising background; your work is on-site conversion and content, not order fulfillment or store operations. Also, the role wants 8+ years with at least three leading a team; your tenure and team size (4 merchandisers) aren't confirmed to meet that bar. Treat this as a stretch application, not a clean match.

Write a second resume summary for Bastien Moreau, leading with the scope of what he plans: he plans schedules for 62 aircraft across 148 city pairs at a mid-size domestic carrier, and owns slot filings for two constrained airports with 100% on-time submission over 9 seasons.

Network planning analyst who plans schedules for 62 aircraft across 148 city pairs at a mid-size domestic carrier. Owns slot filings for two constrained airports, with 100% on-time submission over 9 seasons.

Applying to Havenridge County Human Services for Senior Grants and Contracts Administrator. JD wants subrecipient monitoring including risk assessment and on-site review documentation. My current bullet: "Monitors 23 subrecipients including annual risk assessments and 9 on-site reviews a year." Make it land better for this one.

Monitors 23 subrecipients including annual risk assessments and 9 on-site reviews a year — subrecipient monitoring and on-site review documentation this senior administrator role lists as a core requirement.

Write cover_proof (≤80 words) for Colby Iyengar applying to the Analyst, Portfolio Reporting role at Cordwain Financial. JD requirement: 'maintain automated pipelines behind delinquency and loss curves' with 'precision under deadline.' Persona fact: Colby automated the school department's grade-report generation with Python, cutting a 5-hour monthly task to 15 minutes. Tie the fact to the requirement, one proof point only.

I automated my department's grade-report generation with Python, cutting a 5-hour monthly task down to 15 minutes, without losing accuracy on deadline day. That's the same pipeline discipline Cordwain needs behind its delinquency and loss curves, where the numbers have to be right and on time every single month.

Write a different closing for Anneke Ruzicka's cover letter to Brightloom Foods, this time asking to discuss which shifts still need tier boards and daily management set up. No new claims, 1-2 sentences.
I'd like to hear which shifts still need tier boards and daily management stood up first. Let me know a good time to talk it through.

Trying to punch up this bullet for a Vice President, Commercial application at Windward Voyages: "Launched two new itinerary regions, both reaching target occupancy within one deployment cycle." JD wants deployment planning judgement led jointly with marine operations. Give me a stronger version.
Launched two new itinerary regions, both reaching target occupancy within one deployment cycle — deployment planning judgment this VP role runs jointly with marine operations on every new ship.
Too few results. E-commerce merchandising, Los Angeles. Titles 'senior manager digital merchandising'. Keywords 'PIM, faceted search, taxonomy, GA4, headless'. Locations 'Los Angeles'.

'Headless' and 'faceted search' are words engineers use and merchandising postings rarely do, so they are silently excluding most of your market. Keep 'PIM, taxonomy' and drop the rest. Broaden titles to 'digital merchandising manager, e-commerce manager, category manager, senior manager site merchandising'. Add 'Remote, Orange County' to locations. Three runs a day. Then work the employer list directly: pick fifteen direct-to-consumer and multi-brand retailers whose catalogs resemble yours, check their careers pages every week, and track finds with Add role so the board is not dependent on one scan.

Can you turn this posting into structured data? I need a JSON object with company, title, seniority, location, and requirements. Only the JSON, please — no ```json wrapper.

© 2026 Alderport Financial. All rights reserved. | Privacy Policy | Terms of Use

Now hiring — Principal Engineer, Payments Platform at Alderport Financial
Remote (US)
Level: Senior individual contributor

Qualifications:
- Familiarity with PCI-DSS scoping decisions
- Strong Go or Java; we are mostly Go, and we will not care if you learned it last year as long as the systems thinking is there
- Ten years building backend systems, several of them in payments, banking, or another domain where being wrong costs money

You might also like: other open roles on our team

{"company": "Alderport Financial", "title": "Principal Engineer, Payments Platform", "seniority": "Senior individual contributor", "location": "Remote (US)", "requirements": ["Familiarity with PCI-DSS scoping decisions", "Strong Go or Java; we are mostly Go, and we will not care if you learned it last year as long as the systems thinking is there", "Ten years building backend systems, several of them in payments, banking, or another domain where being wrong costs money"]}
Nothing is moving. 15 cards, all Sourced, none applied. Controls and automation technician in Toledo. Titles 'controls technician'. I run the search when I get home and add whatever it finds.
No more adding cards tonight. Check which of the 15 postings are still live, close the dead ones, and apply to the best two before you run search again. The issue is movement, not volume.

For my Rowanmere Health Technologies Director of Client Experience cover letter, write the closing 1-2 sentences. The posting says the role runs quarterly business reviews for the twenty largest health system relationships. Ask specifically to discuss those reviews.
I'd appreciate a conversation about how quarterly business reviews for your twenty largest health system relationships are structured today.

Considering a home infusion operations role and want help narrowing my skills list. Full list:

clinical operations, Epic (Ambulatory, Cadence), staffing and scheduling, patient throughput, Lean healthcare, root cause analysis, Joint Commission readiness, infection control, nurse retention programs, capacity planning, budget ownership, quality metrics (HCAHPS), policy authoring, interdisciplinary rounding, vendor negotiation, change management, RN licensure (Ohio)

The role is Operations Manager, Home Infusion at Wexley Care Partners in Columbus, hybrid, covering about 2,600 active patients and 70 field nurses across two states. They want 5 years in healthcare operations with at least two managing clinical or field staff, scheduling and capacity planning for a mobile workforce, experience rebuilding a workflow that crosses pharmacy, clinical, and intake teams, familiarity with infusion therapy or home health regulatory requirements, comfort owning productivity and quality dashboards, and a real focus on retention since field nurse turnover is their biggest cost driver. Which skills are relevant here?
["clinical operations", "staffing and scheduling", "capacity planning", "quality metrics (HCAHPS)", "nurse retention programs", "change management", "RN licensure (Ohio)"]

everything on my board has gone quiet and I hate opening it. cinderpeak resorts SVP revenue and distribution, conversation, A-, 86%, 33 days, ghost risk high, docs ready. wanderfare group senior manager revenue science, applied, B, 64%, 41 days, high, docs ready. loftwren senior manager pricing and analytics, applied, B, 61%, 38 days, medium, yes. sablewood resorts group director of revenue strategy, researched, B+, 77%, 22 days, low, no.

Thirty-three quiet days at Cinderpeak is the number that matters, and it's still your best role — A-, 86%, docs already built. Go around the recruiter today and ask a cruise contact for a warm intro to the commercial lead.
Sablewood is second: B+, 77%, 22 days in researched, no docs. Build them and apply this week.
Wanderfare and Loftwren are both B-grade, 64% and 61%, past 38 days with everything already sent. Two down-level roles don't earn a fourth touch. Stop spending on them.

Closing lines for my cover letter to Wanderfare Group's Senior Manager, Revenue Science role. The posting mentions leading a team of three analysts, growing to five. Write a 1-2 sentence closing with a specific ask about that.
How the team of three analysts is structured today, and your plans for growing it to five, is a conversation I'd welcome having. Happy to talk whenever works for you.

I'm a Compliance Manager for consumer lending at a fintech in Salt Lake City, running the compliance program for an unsecured personal-loan product that originates $900M a year across 34 states. I closed a CFPB matter with no civil money penalty by delivering a 41-item remediation plan on schedule, and I led a state licensing expansion into 9 new states in 11 months with zero exam findings. I manage 6 compliance analysts and an $800K co-sourced testing budget. There's a new Director, Regulatory Affairs opening at a $9B community bank holding company in Denver, reporting to the General Counsel, that wants someone with about eight years at director or deputy level owning full examination cycles, plus deposit-side regulation experience with Reg E, Reg DD, and overdraft practices, and the ability to write for a board audience. Worth pursuing?

Strong fit on exam management and consumer regulatory work: your CFPB matter closure and 41-item remediation plan map directly to the exam-cycle ownership they want, and the 9-state licensing expansion with zero findings backs up director-level judgment. Two real gaps: the JD wants deposit-side exposure (Reg E, Reg DD, overdraft), and your record is lending-side; and the role is director-level with a board audience, while your title is Manager, not deputy or director, so this is a seniority stretch, not a lateral move. Worth applying, but be ready to address both directly in the first conversation.

Write cover_open (≤50 words, 1-2 sentences) for Colby Iyengar, a Kansas City high school math teacher applying to the Data Analyst I, Lending opening at Prairiestone Credit Group. Hook on this line from the posting: 'Would rather hire someone who reasons carefully and writes clearly than someone with two extra years of tooling experience; has hired career changers into this team twice before.' No resume recap, no generic openers like 'I am excited to apply.'

Prairiestone has hired career changers into its analytics team twice before, and I'd like to be the third: a math teacher who reasons carefully with data and writes clearly enough that a room full of non-statisticians follows along.

Here's my background and a job posting - how do I compare? Warehouse Systems Analyst, 780,000 sq ft fulfillment center, 52,000 units/day. I wrote 40 SQL reports replacing a nightly manual extract, led labor management system configuration with engineered standards on 14 activity types, and troubleshoot RF and conveyor integration issues as the site's first escalation point on both shifts. Skills: SQL, labor management systems, RF and barcode scanning, cross-functional troubleshooting, process documentation. The role: Senior Manager, Fulfillment Systems at Tarnwell Logistics Group - standardizing WMS configuration across five buildings, owning the labor standards program and its annual re-study cycle, leading a team of four analysts.

Meaningful overlap: your labor management system configuration across 14 activity types and your SQL reporting work directly support their labor standards program and reporting requirements. Being the site's first RF and conveyor escalation point also shows the troubleshooting judgment they need across five buildings. Gap: this role requires people leadership over at least two analysts, and nothing in your background shows managing others - you're an individual contributor and first escalation point, not a team lead. It also wants multi-site experience; your work is confined to one facility. Frame this as a leadership-track application, not a same-level move.

Here is my full bullet list, followed by a role I'm targeting. Return only a JSON array of the bullet IDs I should keep for this JD. Do not rank them.

1. Modeled and recommended exit from 4 underperforming routes; the reallocated capacity returned 6 points of margin.
2. Automated the monthly competitive capacity report, saving about 20 analyst hours a month.
3. Plans schedules for 62 aircraft across 148 city pairs at a mid-size domestic carrier.
4. Owns the slot filings for two constrained airports, with 100% on-time submission over 9 seasons.
5. Improved aircraft utilization from 9.4 to 10.6 block hours a day.
6. Cut schedule-change churn 27% by introducing a 90-day freeze window agreed with crew planning and maintenance.
7. Produced the fleet-gauge study that supported a 12-aircraft order decision presented to the board.
8. Rebuilt the summer schedule to add 3 daily rotations without additional aircraft, worth $11M in incremental annual revenue.
9. Built the connection-bank optimizer in Python that cut average connect time at the primary hub by 14 minutes.
10. Works the daily irregular-operations recovery desk during winter events, coordinating with crew scheduling and dispatch.

Needs: 6-10 years in revenue management, network planning, or pricing. Role: own the demand forecast used for commercial planning.

Which bullets should I highlight for this application, ranked from most to least relevant?

verity ridge health network / clinical informatics analyst / interview / A- / 84% / 8 days since the first round with the hiring manager / ghost medium / docs yes
she kept probing whether lab bench work translates to informatics and I don't think I answered it well

Eight days after a first round is late enough to ask and early enough to matter. Email the hiring manager today and use it to close the gap she probed: "One thing I under-explained - I've been the person who validates instrument data before it ever reaches a clinician. That's the same job from a different chair." Then ask what the next step looks like.

Nothing's moved in a month and I've been telling myself the holidays did it.

| Company | Title | Stage | Fit | Match | Days | Ghost | Docs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Marchand Outfitters | Vice President, Retail Operations | applied | A- | 84% | 29 | high | yes |
| Wickham Row | Director of E-commerce Operations | applied | B+ | 77% | 33 | high | yes |
| Halvard & Pike | Senior Manager, Digital Merchandising | researched | B | 68% | 25 | medium | no |
| Tallow Creek Market Group | Director of Field Operations | sourced | A | 89% | 21 | low | no |
| Ironbark Fulfillment | Vice President of Operations | sourced | B+ | 76% | 30 | medium | no |

The holidays did not do it. Both roles you actually applied to are past four weeks at high ghost risk, which means they were decided without you.
Tallow Creek Market Group is where the month should have gone: A at 89%, low ghost risk, untouched for 21 days. Research and apply this week.
Ironbark Fulfillment at B+ and 76% is the second application, but medium risk at 30 days means write those docs now, not in another fortnight.
Halvard & Pike is a step down in scope at B and 68%. Skip it and keep the board short.

I'm tailoring my resume for the Director, Regulatory Affairs opening at Halewood Bancorp. The posting wants complaint management named as part of the consumer regulatory perimeter this director owns. Here's my bullet: "Stood up a complaint-management workflow that reduced average response time from 21 days to 6." Sharpen it for this JD.
Stood up a complaint-management workflow that reduced average response time from 21 days to 6 — complaint management this director role owns across the whole consumer regulatory perimeter.

I hold an MHA and I've passed two unannounced Joint Commission surveys with zero condition-level findings while managing two ambulatory infusion centers. There's a Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs opening covering 19 post-acute and ambulatory sites under three different licensure categories, reporting to the Chief Quality Officer, that wants accreditation experience across more than one regulatory body — CMS, Joint Commission, or state agencies. How do I compare?

The MHA and the two clean Joint Commission surveys are real credentials that match what they're asking for on the education and accreditation front. Risk: without a second regulatory body on your record, interviewers may question whether you can build one survey-readiness program that actually works across all three licensure types, not just the kind of site you know.
